2606.27617.title: Masked Language Flow Models
2606.27617.paper_summary: Masked Diffusion Models (MDMs) promise fast, parallel language generation, but their reverse transition factorises across token positions -- an approximation that breaks down in the few-step sampling regime where parallel generation ought to provide the greatest efficiency gains. Flow Language Models (FLMs) sidestep this limitation by learning a continuous flow that transports noise toward clean sequences represented in Euclidean space, inducing a flow map that can be distilled for single-step generation. However, this makes complex tasks requiring multi-step reasoning problematic for FLMs, as FLMs are forced to decode every token during generation. To address this, we introduce Masked Language Flow Models (MLFMs), which incorporate masking into FLMs using a continuous stochastic interpolant to bridge partially masked and clean sequences. This design enables conditional generation via continuous flows and allows pretrained MDMs to be converted into MLFMs through a simple, lightweight adaptation. Leveraging this flexibility, we propose a novel sampler that alternates continuous denoising with the discrete unmasking of confident tokens to better support multi-step reasoning. We evaluate our approach on GSM8K and MT-Bench and find, for the first time, that flow-based language models can be scaled to solve downstream reasoning and instruction-following tasks.
2606.27617.paper_summary_zh: 被遮蔽的擴散模型（MDMs）承諾快速、平行的語言生成，但其反向轉換在標記位置上進行因式分解——這種近似在幾步採樣的情況下失效，因為平行生成應該提供最大的效率增益。流語言模型（FLMs）通過學習一種連續流來避開這一限制，該流將噪聲傳輸到在歐幾里得空間中表示的乾淨序列，從而誘導出可以提煉的流圖以實現單步生成。然而，這使得需要多步推理的複雜任務對FLMs來說變得棘手，因為FLMs在生成過程中被迫解碼每個標記。為了解決這個問題，我們引入了被遮蔽的語言流模型（MLFMs），這些模型通過使用連續隨機插值將遮蔽與乾淨序列橋接，將遮蔽整合到FLMs中。這一設計通過連續流實現條件生成，並允許預訓練的MDMs通過簡單、輕量的適應轉換為MLFMs。利用這種靈活性，我們提出了一種新型採樣器，交替進行連續去噪和自信標記的離散去遮蔽，以更好地支持多步推理。我們在GSM8K和MT-Bench上評估了我們的方法，首次發現基於流的語言模型可以擴展到解決下游推理和遵循指令的任務。
2606.27617.author: Iskander Azangulov et.al.
2606.27617.authors: Iskander Azangulov, Kianoosh Ashouritaklimi, Leo Zhang, Simon Vary, Patrick Rebeschini
